seoTitle: Point Boston vs Point Lowly — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Point Boston and Point Lowly. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Point Boston vs Point Lowly: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Point Boston and Point Lowly.
heading: Point Boston vs Point Lowly — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Point Boston, South Australia and Point Lowly, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Point Boston has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Point Lowly (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Point Boston or Point Lowly safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Point Boston has a flood risk score of 49/100, while Point Lowly scores 27/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Point Boston scores 42/100 for bushfire risk. Point Lowly scores 59/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Point Boston or Point Lowly?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Point Boston has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.55% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
